files_changed.summary: Unified token sanitization module — single regex covering 9 token patterns, replaces 4 duplicated patterns
Wired stub handlers to GmailClient via _create_client() + _make_handler() pattern with error sanitization
Wired stub handlers to GitHubClient, added _make_action_handler with try/except + sanitize
Connection pooling — persistent httpx.AsyncClient stored as instance variable
Connection pooling — persistent httpx.AsyncClient with follow_redirects=True
Added timeout param (300s), error parameter handling, asyncio.wait_for, server_close cleanup
Added sanitize() to final error handler — last defense against token leaks in unhandled exceptions
Updated imports to private handler names, error tests now go through _make_handler
Updated imports to private handler names, error tests now go through _make_handler

decisions.decision: Handler wrapping pattern (_make_handler) > inline error handling
decisions.rationale: Client injection + None check + try/except + sanitize in one wrapper — DRY, consistent error boundary
Handlers are internal implementation; only register() and _make_handler are the public contract
alternatives_rejected: Public handlers: test convenience, but exposes internal interface

decisions.decision: Shared sanitize module > per-file regex patterns
decisions.rationale: Single source of truth prevents pattern drift — adding a new token type updates all callers
alternatives_rejected: Per-file patterns: faster initial write, but 4 files already drifted

decisions.rationale: Even if a handler forgets to sanitize, the MCP server catches and sanitizes all unhandled exceptions

lessons: QR gate caught _make_action_handler missing error handling — identical pattern to _make_handler but without try/except. Defense-in-depth requires ALL error paths, not just the obvious ones
Renaming public functions to private breaks downstream tests — should have updated tests in same commit as rename
Error handling tests must exercise the actual wrapper (_make_handler), not raw handlers — testing raw handlers misses the sanitization behavior
Phase 1 Standard tier QR (1 iteration, MUST+SHOULD) was sufficient — found 2 SHOULD items, both fixable in-session
unresolved: No direct unit tests for shared/sanitize.py — core security module needs its own test suite
httpx.AsyncClient lifecycle: clients are created at import time but never explicitly closed (aclose)
No token refresh flow integration: _create_client loads token once, doesn't handle mid-session expiry
next_steps: Add test_sanitize.py with coverage for all 9 token patterns
Add AsyncClient.aclose() cleanup in client classes or context manager pattern
Consider token refresh integration in _create_client or _make_handler
